_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Pool chemicals are SDS-listed dangerous goods; handling must follow supplier guidance.

hazards.title: Pool chemical exposure (acid, chlorine, salt)
hazards.risk: Acid burns, chlorine inhalation, alkali burns from balancing chemicals

controls.title: SDS for every chemical on site
Never mix chemicals; always add chemical to water, not water to chemical
P3 respirator if handling powdered chlorine in confined area

hazards.title: Drowning — pool filling
hazards.risk: Pool filling for first time; barrier may not yet be certified compliant

controls.title: No water introduced until barrier installed and compliance inspection passed OR temporary equivalent barrier in place
Pool not left unsupervised during fill if barrier not yet compliant

hazards.title: Electric shock during equipment start-up
hazards.risk: First energise of pump / heater / chlorinator — RCD / bonding faults could manifest

controls.title: Licensed electrician present for first energise; RCD test before pool entry
No one in water during first energise

tasks: Pre-fill: confirm barrier compliance certificate issued OR effective temporary barrier in place; pool isolated from public access.
Confirm Electrical CoC + Gas Type A (if applicable) issued.
Fill via hose from owner's mains; monitor fill progress.
First energise of pump with electrician present; RCD trip test; verify no leakage.
Run pump for 24–48 hours circulation before first chemical addition.
Test water chemistry: pH, alkalinity, calcium hardness, chlorine, salt (if salt pool).
Add balancing chemicals in correct order per SDS; allow circulation time between additions.
Final handover test: all controls operational, chemistry balanced, barrier compliant.
Owner handover briefing: operating procedure, chemical safety, warranty, maintenance schedule.
Handover pack uploaded to Hub: equipment manuals, CoCs, barrier compliance, chemistry start values.
